runs.llm_reason: Facing a pot-size flop bet on JcAd5s in position with deep stacks, the default low-risk response is to continue with strong made hands, pair-plus-draws, and good broadway continuations. Without hero cards or solver output, calling preserves range advantage and avoids overinflating a deep-SPR pot on a board where the bettor can still be wide.

Facing a pot-sized flop bet on JcAd5s in position with deep stacks, the safest visible-context response is to continue only with a made hand or strong draw. Without hero cards, the pot-sized wager suggests significant pressure on a board that favors the bettor enough to use a large size. Calling preserves flexibility and avoids over-committing with unknown equity; raising would be too aggressive without hand info, and folding is too strong absent a specific weak holding.

Facing a pot-sized flop bet in position on JcAd5s with deep stacks, the default low-risk and legal response is to continue selectively rather than inflate the pot without hand information. The board is relatively dry but interacts strongly with Ax/Jx, so without knowing hero cards there is no clear value-raise or bluff-raise basis. Calling preserves position and keeps ranges wide at high SPR.

Facing a pot-sized flop bet on JcAd5s as the in-position player, continuing with a call is the safest default without knowing hero's exact hand. The board is relatively dry and favors the bettor's range somewhat, so raising without card information is not justified; folding would be too tight given the wide range possible in heads-up and deep SPR. Call keeps the pot controlled and preserves positional advantage.

Facing a pot-sized flop bet on JcAd5s with deep stacks and in position, the standard low-risk default is to continue with a calling range rather than inflate the pot without knowing our exact holding. The board is fairly dynamic but not so coordinated that an immediate raise is mandatory; calling preserves positional advantage and keeps weaker holdings and bluffs in. Without hero cards, a call is the most flexible legal response.
